raw_input:
[5.0/5] Works good I was impressed by the temperature control and that it actually does heat up the water and fairy fast. Only things I would change is add more bubbles and spread the feet out more, they feel like your feet are cramped in there having to hold your legs together. Would buy again. [4.0/5] This thing is amazing! I was honestly quite surprised about the size ... [[VIDEOID:7cd974a6de47cf93a9edc7773b930b9e]] This thing is amazing! I was honestly quite surprised about the size of it. It's huge and very comparable to what you see at nail salons. It's already set up to use - all you have to do is attach the wheels! It comes with two different foot massage pads which easily snap into place at the bottom of the unit. It's relatively heavy so it's nice that it does have wheels. It stands at about 19" tall. The water level is about 6" so you get a good amount of depth around your feet / ankles/ mid calf range. This thing can also fit a wide variety of sized feet! It feet my husbands size 13 very easily! My six year old daughter loved this as well. The panel is LED touch screen. It's pretty easy to use. Just push whatever function you want. Make sure to read the instruction manual as it is more in depth and really gives more information on how to use the functions. You can have bubbles - the massage rollers going - the fountain! - and of course - the heater to warm the water. That does take a while to heat the water so I recommend adding warm water already to cut down on the amount of time spent waiting. Overall, this is a really nice foot spa! Great value. [1.0/5] Heating element did not work, or digital panel. Defective so returned & have to pay return shipping. Very disappointed Heating element did not work, nor did digital pad work properly. Very disappointed. Item was defective yet
I have to pay for return shipping. If it was a question of I had changed my mind I have no problem paying return
shipping, but this was defective. Know that if you purchase and return this seller does not pay return shipping
costs on defects. [5.0/5] Five Stars Awesome [2.0/5] This item is too heavy to pull. I couldn't ... This item is too heavy to pull. I couldn't empty the water out. The hose is too short so I had to pull it outside to empty it. The instruments meant to massage your feet, do not work as I expected. [5.0/5] Good machine for Big sore feet Works great! Be careful with the tab that hold the drain spout in place. I pulled on it and it broke, you're actually supposed to lift up on it before pulling it out. The machine operates well. Good heat and I like the two different stiles of foot massage options that come included. [5.0/5] Great machine. Warms water quickly OMG, Great machine. Warms water quickly, has a number of features ranging from salt holder, water fountain, and best of all, 2 auto foot massage pads. Don't need to do anything but sit back and enjoy the pads do the work. [5.0/5] Home Spa Heaven ~ [[VIDEOID:a4a584a64c290ec35e3eb063c37e5b8d]] I recently was able to purchase the LuxorWare Foot Spa Massager sold by Luxor Outlet in a gorgeous coffee/cream color scheme and let me tell you, it is amazing. It is so easy to use ~ it basically comes out of the box ready to use...All I had to do was pop on the wheels & add water and it was ready to go. I was worried it would be difficult to use but I can honestly say that it is beyond user friendly...each function has a separate button making it extremely easy to customize for the perfect foot massage.
So let me give you the details ~
It comes with motorized roller covers (you actually get two styles of interchangeable massage plates) that hit acupressure points in your feet, water circulation and heat (adjustable) therapy, air bubbles and water jets to stimulate foot reflex zone, an Aqua Massage from calf to toe) and a super easy to use splash proof touch panel with LED screen display
You can also adjust the temp and set different timers and add essential oils. This is the most complete home foot spa I have ever seen. I have used it probably every other night since I purchased ~ my feet have never felt so pampered!! [5.0/5] Great spa! I am loving this foot spa. [4.0/5] Great for the money, just wish all the rollers moved. Nice foot spa and easy to use. I like that it comes with two sets of foot rollers, I just wish all the rollers moved as opposed to two of the four. I do not think the red LED does anything therapeutic. I have size 11 US feet and there is plenty of room to feel comfortable. [5.0/5] Love it! Best foot spa I have ever used! I bought it for my husband and he loves it too. Lots of room and almost just as good as going to the salon for a pedicure. [5.0/5] Definitely a 5 Star. This is a great foot spa. The water heats up fairly fast and stays hot. It has a variety of choices for temperature controls and timer so I can control the degree of heat I want. I have recommended this LuxorWare high end model to many friends and relatives. Love it. [4.0/5] Noisy. Otherwise ok Thought it would be a good idea to have since my husbands feet ache a lot.
Once I researched how to stop the constant beeping (unplug while running then plug back it, and start up again.. many times over) but now it squeaks all the time like it needs to be oiled. (??)
It’s honestly to cumbersome for me (52yo female) to drain and clean. Weighs a lot (obviously) when full. But if you're hard of hearing this might be perfect for you. It heats up without issues and otherwise functions fine. [4.0/5] I like it; however I like it; however, it has one small disadvantage. The unit is rather tall and if you are tall, i.e., more than 5'8", or you have a low couch/chair you will be in a very awkward position not to mention get tired soon (your back will tell you that :-)
My suggestion would have been to create depressions in the tub so that the leg will rest comfortably. [5.0/5] Five Stars This is clearly the best in the market. My wife and I are very thankful to this little wonder. [5.0/5] Robust, many features, lots of pros and few cons. I had a HoMedics foot spa that I bought from a big box drugstore when I had a foot infection three years ago. It broke after 3 uses. But I liked it, and wanted to replace it with one that was more robustly built, had a reliable water heater, and came with good foot massage tools.
After a lot of research, I settled on the LuxorWare Foot Spa Massager. I've used it a dozen times (my husband has used it, too), and I'm so happy I bought it! Here are the reasons I love it:
• It is heavy and well built. The plastic seems thick and strong, and it seems pretty well constructed, especially the foot massage tools.
• The tub is deep. When you fill it to the maximum fill line, it comes up a few inches above the ankle.
• The water heater is excellent, going from 86 degrees F to 115+ degrees F (I haven't maxed out on the heat, as 103–105 degrees F is super warm). And the heat stays where you set it without variation unless you dial it up or down.
• The timer goes in 10-minute increments and beeps at you when you have 5 minutes left, so you can put down your book and get your towel ready. A nice feature I wasn't looking for, but am glad is there.
• The water, while not swirling or bubbling briskly, does move enough for me, and comes from enough directions to work on the entire foot.
• I use scented Epsom salts in it, and they work fine. Every other time I use the unit, I put a couple of inches of hot water with a tiny bit of dish detergent, insert a pair of foot massager tools, and turn the motor on briefly to get the lightly sudsed water to run through all the openings (the buttons on top of the unit operate different squirting gizmos). I follow this with a freshwater rinse and the unit is clean again.
•Best thing: There are 2 sets of foot massaging tools, that pop in and out easily (I've only changed them with an empty unit before I fill it, so I don't know whether they can be changed with water in the tub.) They both work well and are vigorous enough to really stimulate and massage my feet. I like the vigorous massage, but if you just want a gentle massage, you can simply rest your feet on the devices and they'll feel very mild and gentle.
Here are the cons, though in my opinion the plusses far outweigh them:
– The casters are small and don't roll easily, especially on a rug. I have to use the handle and pull the unit along carefully by the rim to scoot it across the floor. It's not that it feels heavy (though it is, so tall and full of water); it's just awkward to shuffle it along.
– Because it's so tall, it holds a lot of water, and I need a bit of strength to heft it up onto the edge of the bathtub to empty it. The drain spout is down at the bottom of the unit, which makes sense, but you have to get the unit high enough to actually drain the water out into some receptacle, which would have to be so low to the ground that it would be difficult to get all the water out efficiently. So I do make the effort to lift the whole unit up on the bathtub edge and dump it in (pouring over the non-motor edge!).
– This one I learned the hard way: The handle is only for PULLING the unit gently across the floor. Not for lifting it! When the unit has water to the max fill line, the handle does not lift the load from the middle and actually TIPS the water out the front (onto the rug or floor). So just use the handle for a guide, not to lift.
Bottom line: I would definitely recommend this product and would buy it again.

response:
Customer reviews for LuxorWare Foot Spa Massager. Most customers happy. Here what cave people say:

GOOD THINGS: Water heat fast, stay hot. Good temperature control, timer work well. Two different foot massage pad options, easy swap. Tub big, fit size 13 foot, fit child foot too. Bubbles, jets, rollers, fountain all work. Easy use, touch screen simple. Come ready use, just add water and wheels. Good value, like salon experience at home.

BAD THINGS: One unit defective, heating element and panel not work. Seller make customer pay return shipping, customer very angry. Unit heavy, hard move, hard empty. Drain spout not work well, hard empty water. One customer say massage tools not work as expected. Unit tall, awkward sit if have low couch. Beep loud, squeak, need oil. Caster wheel small, not roll good on rug.

TIPS FROM CUSTOMERS: Add warm water first, cut heating time. Handle only for guiding, not lifting when full or water spill everywhere. Use dish soap and run motor briefly to clean inside. Epsom salt work fine in unit.

OVERALL: Most give 4-5 stars. One very unhappy with defective unit. Strong recommend from majority of buyers.
